title: Extract rules fallback text into a dedicated packaged file
summary: This ticket is complete. The long `.rules` fallback heredoc was removed from `zed-agentops-init.sh` and replaced with a dedicated packaged fallback file, `src/agentops_mcp_server/workflow_rules_fallback.txt`. The scaffold still prefers the canonical rules generated from `workflow_rules.py` when available, while the fallback path now copies the packaged fallback resource instead of duplicating the full rules text inline in the shell script.
outputs: A dedicated packaged fallback rules file used by scaffold generation instead of a long inline shell heredoc
Reduced duplication between canonical workflow rules and fallback scaffold-generation content
Regression coverage confirming fallback rules packaging and scaffold behavior remain aligned
acceptance_criteria: The long fallback `.rules` text previously embedded in `zed-agentops-init.sh` is extracted into a dedicated file in the packaged project resources.
The normal scaffold-generation path continues to use the canonical rules generated from `workflow_rules.py` when that source is available.
The fallback scaffold-generation path no longer duplicates the full `.rules` content inline inside `zed-agentops-init.sh`.
The fallback file is packaged and available in the installed distribution wherever `zed-agentops-init.sh` depends on it.
Scaffold generation behavior remains correct for both canonical-source and fallback-source cases.
The change reduces maintenance drift risk without weakening the existing rules canonicalization approach.
Repository verification passed after the fallback-file extraction changes were implemented.
tasks: Identify the current fallback `.rules` heredoc in `zed-agentops-init.sh` and define the minimal file-based extraction needed to remove inline duplication.
Create a dedicated packaged fallback rules file and place it in a location consistent with the current packaging strategy.
Update `zed-agentops-init.sh` so the fallback path reads or copies the packaged fallback file instead of embedding the full rules text inline.
Preserve the existing preferred path that generates `.rules` from `workflow_rules.py` when the canonical Python source is available.
Update packaging configuration so the fallback rules file is present in distributed artifacts.
Add or extend tests that confirm the packaged fallback file exists and that scaffold generation resources remain aligned.
Run repository verification and record concise evidence that the extraction did not change the intended workflow contract.
notes: This is a maintenance and drift-reduction follow-up, not a change to the canonical workflow contract itself.
Keep `workflow_rules.py` as the canonical source of workflow rules.
The dedicated fallback file exists only to support scaffold generation when the canonical Python source is unavailable.
The shell script now uses `workflow_rules_fallback.txt` for the fallback path and no longer embeds the full `.rules` body inline.
Packaging and regression checks were updated so the fallback file is included in distributed artifacts and the old inline heredoc does not silently return.
